text:
## 📋 Summary

This PR adds a small, self-contained `string_utils.js` module with three pure string helpers (`truncate`, `collapseWhitespace`, `equalsIgnoreCase`). The code is clean, well-documented, and the implementation matches the documented contracts — no defects found.

## 🔍 Findings

_No Critical, High, or Medium issues identified._

🟢 Low
- **`string_utils.js:62` — `equalsIgnoreCase` rejects boxed `String` objects.** `typeof new String("x")` is `"object"`, so a boxed string will be treated as non-string and return `false` even when paired with an equal primitive. Unlikely to matter in practice (and arguably the safer default), but worth a one-line JSDoc note if you ever want to accept boxed strings.

## ✅ Positives

- Defensive input validation is consistent across functions: `truncate` and `collapseWhitespace` throw informative `TypeError`/`RangeError`s, while `equalsIgnoreCase` is explicitly documented as non-throwing — and the implementation matches that contract exactly.
- `truncate` correctly handles the `max === 1` edge case (returns a single ellipsis) so the result length never exceeds `max` even at the boundary.
- `Number.isInteger(max) || max < 1` correctly rejects `NaN`, `Infinity`, and floats — avoiding the silent wraparound class of bugs called out in the review brief.
- No global state, no I/O, no hidden dependencies; exports are explicit via `module.exports`.
- Good JSDoc on all three functions, including the non-obvious "ellipsis included" semantics for `truncate`.

Overall: looks good to merge. (For higher confidence, a tiny test file covering the edge cases above — empty input, `max === 1`, `max === input.length`, non-ASCII in `equalsIgnoreCase` — would be a nice follow-up, but is not blocking.)
